title: uflabdbm
text: uflabdbm Get a list of the names of all database fields in an entity. Explain this code short uflabdbm (unsigned char* entity, unsigned char* buffer ); Copy Parameters entity Name of the entity. To resolve ambiguity, the name can be fully qualified, for example field . entity . You can also leave it empty to denote the current entity. buffer A comma-separated list of uppercase field names. Ensure that this buffer is large enough to hold the anticipated data. (Output) Return Values Zero—this function always returns zero. If there is an error, the buffer will remain empty. This function has no error handling. It always returns zero. If there is an error in the entity name, there is no error message and the buffer will simply remain empty. If the buffer is too small to hold the result, the application will probably crash. Use Library: yrtl Description uflabdbm gets the names of all the database fields in the specified entity and places them in buffer as a comma-separated list of uppercase names, for example, "NAME,STREET,NUMBER,ZIP,CITY". Non-database fields in the entity will not be included. The entity parameter can be unqualified (for example, "PERSON"), qualified ("PERSON . PAYROLL"), or empty ("") or zero to denote the current entity. The following example gets the names of the fields in the PERSON entity: Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> char buffer[500]; /* Insure that this is large enough */ (void)uflabdbm("person.payroll", buffer); Copy Following a call to this function, buffer might contain the following: Explain this code NAME,PERSNUMBER,ADDRESS,ZIP,CITY Copy Was this topic helpful?
